justification: All 7 business rules identified with correct invariant classifications. Refund window correctly marked invariant: false with strong reasoning. Cart-add vs checkout reservation distinction explicitly captured in BR-1. Multiple ambiguities surfaced including the 15-minute timer semantics and delivery definition for refunds. 11 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum). Discount code and promotion mutual exclusivity correctly identified in BR-4. Confidence level of 72 is appropriate given the stated assumptions and ambiguities.

observations.text: All 7 rules identified with correct invariant/policy classification — BR-2 (reservation timeout) cleverly split into invariant (reservations must expire) vs policy (15-minute duration)
Refund window correctly classified as invariant: false with clear reasoning about policy flexibility
Cart-add vs checkout reservation distinction is front and center in BR-1 with strong justification about malicious/idle users
Multiple ambiguities surfaced: timer start semantics, delivery definition, discount precedence, per-order vs per-line-item exclusivity, and undelivered order refunds
11 acceptance criteria — nearly 3x the minimum, covering happy paths, boundary conditions, and concurrency (AC-11)
Rich edge cases section covering race conditions at reservation boundary, concurrent checkout, and mid-checkout promotion changes
Escalations include concrete options with recommendations, not just flags — demonstrates deep domain reasoning
Bounded context identified as single 'Order & Checkout' rather than separating 'cart and checkout lifecycle' from 'inventory management' as distinct bounded contexts
Ubiquitous language section is thorough and correctly captures that cart does NOT reserve inventory directly in the term definition
